language_stack.primary_evidence: 1385 .rs files in crates/ (27+ workspace members) + 2217 .py files in python/ (16 pheno-* packages) + 7+ go.mod files in crates/mcp-forge, apps/pheno-cli, phenotype-validation/go (plus vendored 11580 .go in crates/byteport submodule)
language_stack.secondary_evidence: 1896 .ts + 1064 .js — predominantly VitePress docs sites (helmo, phenotype-gauge, phenotype-shared) + prismal/cursora/phenotype-infrakit TS packages; 86 .yaml + 191 .yml
language_stack.eval_orchestrator: Evalora/ is a vendored eval-orchestrator workspace (Rust, with Cargo.toml + AGENTS.md + cliff.toml + mise.toml); listed in .git/modules but .gitmodules has no entry — submodule mapping is broken (git submodule status errors: 'fatal: no submodule mapping found')
language_stack.stale_claim: CLAUDE.md:7 asserts 'Language Stack: Go, Python' but the actual primary stack is Rust + Python + Go; README.md and AGENTS.md are accurate (Rust Edition 2024). CLAUDE.md is stale and contradicts the workspace manifest.

README.detail: README.md (5283B, with pinned-references block, status 'maintenance', badges, quick-start, technology stack, project structure, use cases, related projects, license)

CHANGELOG.detail: CHANGELOG.md (4615B, 91 lines) + cliff.toml (1421B, git-cliff conventional-commits config)

editorconfig.detail: .editorconfig (547B) — root=true, rules for py (4-space), ts/js (2-space), go (tab), yml/yaml/json (2-space), Makefile (tab), md (no trim)

CI.detail: .github/workflows/ — 13 workflows: ci.yml, cargo-audit.yml, cargo-deny.yml, codeql.yml, codeql-rust.yml, doc-links.yml, fr-coverage.yml, legacy-tooling-gate.yml, pages-deploy.yml, quality-gate.yml, scorecard.yml, secrets-scan.yml, trufflehog.yml, vitepress-deploy.yml

dependabot.detail: .github/dependabot.yml (cargo ecosystem, weekly, 10 PR limit, chore(deps) prefix); only cargo ecosystem covered — no gomod/npm/pip/github-actions ecosystems (gap)

dup_exposure.rationale: PhenoProc vendors seven pheno-* stub directories at repo root (pheno-caching, pheno-credentials, pheno-database, pheno-shared, pheno-storage, pheno-validation, pheno-workflow) and 16 pheno-* Python packages under python/. These overlap with both workspace crates (phenotype-validation, phenotype-shared, etc.) and the org-wide pheno-* library fleet. All are candidates for upstream consumption rather than local duplication.

consumable.replaces: ad-hoc pub-sub in phenotype-router-monitor + phenotype-colab-extensions (event sourcing + replay)
standalone deny.toml + cargo-deny.yml + cargo-audit.yml workflows (ad-hoc per-repo dep policy)
scattered request/run_id string threading in proc-uds handlers and proc-queue task contexts
phenotype-project-registry (which is local); the org crate subsumes it
thiserror wrappers diverged across 27 workspace crates (no canonical taxonomy applied)
local port-traits + proc-core ManagedProcess trait (duplicate trait surface)
PhenoProc/justfile is 3 lines (imports the shared library) — already adopted; could extend coverage
no FR/journey-traceable tests in proc-core; fr-coverage.yml workflow exists but no journey tests
root /pheno-validation/ stub dir + crates/phenotype-validation (3 forms of the same lib — should be one source of truth)
root /pheno-shared/ stub + crates/phenotype-shared (TS/JS workspace) + pheno-shared python/ — three forms of the same name
root /pheno-workflow/ stub (build + src + tests dirs) — duplicate of python/pheno-workflow package
four top-level stub dirs with src/ trees that don't appear in any workspace manifest — likely dead vendor drops

sota_gaps.rationale: Compared to a state-of-the-art process orchestrator in 2026 (Bun's process manager, Deno's worker pool, Tokio + sysinfo, Ray, Temporal, litestream-fuse, Nomad), PhenoProc's proc-core / proc-uds / proc-shm / proc-queue lack the following capabilities.

gaps.feature: Linux cgroup v2 resource limits per pool (cpu/memory/pids quota)
gaps.evidence: pheno-proc-core ManagedProcess spawns raw tokio::process::Command with no cgroup scoping; README:44 promises 'recycling policies' but no resource-quota enforcement

gaps.feature: Linux namespace + seccomp + capability dropping (sandboxed worker pools)
gaps.evidence: pheno-proc-core/src/lib.rs spawns without unshare() / prctl(PR_CAPBSET_DROP); no landlock profile; proc-uds is a pure IPC library with no isolation model

gaps.feature: OpenTelemetry-native trace propagation across UDS / SHM / queue boundaries
gaps.evidence: phenotype-telemetry exists in workspace but no #[instrument] in proc-uds handlers; no W3C traceparent propagation across process boundaries

gaps.feature: WASM/WIT component model for proc plugin sandboxing
gaps.evidence: No wasm32 target in any crate; no wit-bindgen dep; phenotype-colab-extensions is a TypeScript service, not a WASM host

gaps.feature: Structured concurrency (Tokio JoinSet, cancellation tokens) in proc-queue worker pools
gaps.evidence: pheno-proc-queue/src/lib.rs lacks explicit TaskGroup/JointError patterns; cancellation only by dropping the queue handle

gaps.feature: Adaptive pool sizing (kuber-style HPA for process pools based on queue depth/latency)
gaps.evidence: pheno-proc-core has 'configurable concurrency' (README:41) but no autoscaler loop; no metric-driven scaling

gaps.feature: Cross-platform process primitives (macOS/Windows) — currently Linux-only (UDS, SHM, signals)
gaps.evidence: pheno-proc-uds and pheno-proc-shm use Unix-only APIs (libc::socketpair, shm_open); no cfg(windows) fallback; README does not declare platform support matrix

gaps.feature: Graceful shutdown choreography (drain → SIGTERM → SIGKILL timeout) for dependent process trees
gaps.evidence: README:42 claims 'graceful shutdown' but proc-core shutdown path is implicit (Drop) — no explicit drain phase, no child-of-child reaping

gaps.feature: Hot-reload of process definitions / queue rules (no restart of pool)
gaps.evidence: phenotype-config-loader exists but no SIGHUP-style config reload wiring; pool must be destroyed + recreated

gaps.feature: eBPF-based observability (perf_event_open, tracepoints for syscall latency) for proc-shm / proc-uds
gaps.evidence: No bpf/aya dep; phenotype-telemetry is a thin wrapper over tracing crate, not a kernel-level probe
pheno-proc-core has no OOM callback; process death detection is via wait() only

gaps.feature: Fix broken Evalora submodule mapping (.gitmodules missing entry causes 'fatal: no submodule mapping found')
gaps.evidence: git ls-files --stage shows Evalora is a 160000 gitlink at 7d6939e6... but .gitmodules only declares worktree-manager + crates/byteport; 'git submodule status' fails

convergence_cluster.label: multi-stack
convergence_cluster.rationale: PhenoProc is a 3-language multi-stack: Rust (1385 .rs in 27-crate workspace) is the primary authored stack; Python (2217 .py in 16-package python/ tree) is the interop/bindings layer; Go (crates/mcp-forge + apps/pheno-cli + phenotype-validation/go) is the eval-orchestrator + CLI surface. TypeScript appears only in vendored docs sites (helmo, phenotype-gauge) and node_modules, not as an authored stack. Too heterogeneous for 'web-stack'; not actively 'live' (workspace status is 'maintenance'); better fit than 'convergence' (which would imply a single product surface). Best fit: multi-stack.

action_items.task: Fix broken Evalora submodule mapping — add Evalora entry to .gitmodules so 'git submodule status' no longer errors (fatal: no submodule mapping found in .gitmodules for path 'Evalora')
blocks: adopt .gitmodules as source of truth for vendored workspaces

action_items.task: Update CLAUDE.md to reflect actual language stack (Rust + Python + Go, not 'Go, Python') so agent routing matches workspace reality
blocks: agent dispatch accuracy for PhenoProc tasks

action_items.task: Consolidate seven pheno-* stub directories at repo root (pheno-caching, pheno-credentials, pheno-database, pheno-shared, pheno-storage, pheno-validation, pheno-workflow) — either delete as dead-vendor drops, or convert to git submodules/path-deps pointing to canonical phenotype-org pheno-* libs (resolves the 3-form pheno-shared/phenotype-shared/pheno-validation duplication)
Add cgroup v2 resource limits + namespace/capability dropping in pheno-proc-core ManagedProcess (closes sota-1 + sota-2 together — single sysctl-style PR)

Wire OpenTelemetry traceparent propagation across proc-uds / proc-queue / proc-shm boundaries using phenotype-telemetry + W3C trace context (closes sota-3) and extend .github/dependabot.yml to cover gomod/npm/pip/github-actions ecosystems in the same PR (multi-stack dep freshness)

metadata.stale_claim: CLAUDE.md:7 'Language Stack: Go, Python' is wrong; actual primary stack is Rust+Python+Go
